detail.summary: NPI address changed — now practicing in Baker City, OR
detail.student_match: 3 third-year students listed Orthopedic Surgery as primary interest at COMP-Northwest

suggested_outreach.subject: Welcome to Oregon — COMP-Northwest connection
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Dr. Sandefur,

I noticed you're practicing in Baker City — that's beautiful country out there.

I wanted to reach out because COMP-Northwest is just up the road in Lebanon, and we have students rotating through Central Oregon who would benefit enormously from connecting with someone in your specialty.

No pressure at all — but if you'd ever be open to a brief conversation about what a mentorship connection might look like, I'd love to set that up.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest

detail.summary: NPI taxonomy updated — Sports Medicine subspecialty added
detail.student_match: 5 COMP students listed Sports Medicine as primary interest

suggested_outreach.subject: Congratulations on your Sports Medicine certification
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Dr. Charochak,

Congratulations on earning your Sports Medicine board certification. That's a significant achievement, and I wanted to make sure you heard it from us.

I also wanted to mention — we have several current students with a strong interest in Sports Medicine. If you'd ever be open to a conversation with one of them, I know it would mean a great deal.

No obligation at all. Just an open door.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest

detail.summary: Published in JAMA Psychiatry: mindfulness-based interventions
detail.publication: "Integrating Mindfulness-Based Interventions in Community Psychiatric Practice" — JAMA Psychiatry, March 2026
detail.student_match: 7 COMP students listed Psychiatry as primary interest

suggested_outreach.subject: Your JAMA Psychiatry publication — congratulations
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Dr. Kesler,

I saw your recent publication in JAMA Psychiatry on mindfulness-based interventions — congratulations. That's the kind of work that makes me so proud to be connected to our COMP graduates.

I'll be honest, we haven't been in touch as much as we should have been. That's something I'm working to change.

If you'd be open to it, I'd love to explore a few possibilities — a guest lecture for our students, a feature in our alumni spotlight, or simply a conversation about what you're building.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest

detail.summary: Advisory board conversation — promised follow-up in March
detail.context: Met at AOA Conference Jan 15. Expressed interest in advisory board role. You said you'd follow up in March.

suggested_outreach.subject: Following up — advisory board conversation
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Dr. Konecny,

I've been meaning to follow up on our conversation at AOA in January. You mentioned interest in the advisory board, and I wanted to circle back on that.

We're putting together the next cohort and I think your perspective — 35 years of cardiothoracic surgery experience — would be invaluable.

Would you have 20 minutes for a call this month? I'd love to share more about what we're building.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest

detail.context: Class of 2016 will reach their 10-year milestone in 2026. 257 graduates across both campuses.

suggested_outreach.subject: Class of 2016 — your 10-year reunion
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Class of 2016,

It's hard to believe it's been 10 years. A lot has changed — at COMP, in medicine, and I'm sure in your own lives and careers.

We're planning a reunion event and I'd love for you to be part of it. Whether you're nearby or across the country, there's a place for you.

More details coming soon. For now, I just wanted you to know — we remember you, we're proud of you, and the door is always open.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest

detail.summary: Practicing in Newport, OR — near Lebanon campus, zero engagement on record
detail.context: NPI shows Dr. Forsyth practicing Emergency Medicine in Newport, OR — close to COMP-Northwest. No touchpoint on record with COMP.

suggested_outreach.subject: A neighbor and an alumna — coffee sometime?
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Dr. Forsyth,

I realized we have an alumna practicing right near us in Oregon and we've never properly connected. That's on us.

I'd love to buy you a coffee and hear how things are going. No agenda — just genuinely curious how your career has unfolded since COMP.

If you're ever open to it, my door is always open.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest

detail.summary: Match Day 2026 complete — 14 of 15 SOAP students placed. Begin planning for 2027.
detail.context: Marion Safawi coordinated 3 SOAP alumni mentors for Match Day 2026. Results: 14 of 15 unmatched students placed. Begin identifying and recruiting standby mentors for Match Day 2027 (est. March 2027).
Dr. Powell's NPI taxonomy updated to reflect administrative role alongside clinical. Cross-referenced with public announcement. Board candidate material.
detail.last_touchpoint: 4 months ago — guest lecture at Pomona

suggested_outreach.subject: Department chair — well deserved
suggested_outreach.body:
Hi Dr. Powell,

I just heard about your appointment as Department Chair. I can't say I'm surprised — but I am so proud.

You've always gone above and beyond for our students, and this recognition is well deserved. COMP is better because of alumni like you.

I'd love to catch up when you have a moment. There are some exciting things happening here that I think you'd want to be part of.

Warm regards,
Lisa Warren, DO, MBA
Dean, COMP & COMP-Northwest
